message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit() / Editor class. These are the two files my task explicitly touches.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (edit_files, filename accepts str|Iterable[str]|None)

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both our features need the same 2 files (no way around it - edit() lives in termui.py, Editor class in _termui_impl.py). Let's each implement independently: I'm only touching Editor.__init__ (adding process_priority param + storing it) and the subprocess-launch site (setting priority via psutil/os.nice on Unix, subprocess.CREATE_* priority class on Windows), plus adding process_priority kwarg to click.edit() in termui.py, passed through to Editor(...). I won't touch filename/edit_files handling. We'll git merge both branches at the end and resolve any textual conflicts by hand since the changes target different params/lines. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both features must touch the same 2 files (edit() signature + Editor class), true disjoint split isn't possible. Propose: we each implement independently -- I'll add filename: str|Iterable[str]|None handling (edit_files method, quoting multiple paths in subprocess call) touching Editor.edit_files/edit()/get_editor and the edit() wrapper's filename param. You add process_priority to Editor.__init__/edit()/edit_file signatures. We'll keep changes additive/non-overlapping in the same functions where possible, and resolve any merge conflicts when combining branches later. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll add process_priority param only (Editor.__init__, edit()/edit_file signatures + subprocess launch site), staying out of the filename/edit_files multi-path logic. Will merge branches at the end to combine both features. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed: I own filename/edit_files (multi-file support) in Editor + edit() wrapper; you own process_priority param in Editor.__init__/subprocess-launch + edit() wrapper. Will merge branches at the end and hand-resolve any textual conflicts.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Multi-file edit support implemented: Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames), click.edit() filename param accepts str|Iterable[str]|None, added typing overloads, CHANGES.rst entry added. Tests pass (tests/test_termui.py). Ready to merge.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and edit_file() (windows creationflags / unix os.setpriority, best-effort, gracefully ignored on failure), and threaded process_priority through click.edit(). Existing tests still pass. Ready to merge whenever you're done.
